summary: All code_refs and source figures verified against ground truth. fallback_adapter.py line citations (45/71/175 attempt_timeout=5.0, 17-18 max_retry=0, 25/154-199 recovery probe) all hold. Every numeric/quote attribution to vapi-latency confirmed verbatim. The 'mean + k·σ' is correctly framed as the note's own interpretation of the source's 'historical standard deviation' + 'dynamic threshold' language, not falsely quoted.

claims.claim: frontmatter code_ref points to attempt_timeout default
fixed attempt_timeout=5.0 per LLM attempt at lines 45,71,175
max_retry=0 so it fails over rather than retrying same endpoint
claims.found: 17-19 'DEFAULT_FALLBACK_API_CONNECT_OPTIONS = APIConnectOptions(max_retry=0, timeout=...)'; also max_retry_per_llm=0 default at line 47

claims.claim: background recovering_task probes a downed provider before returning it to rotation
claims.found: 25 'recovering_task: asyncio.Task[None] | None'; 154-199 _try_generate with check_recovery flag is the recovery-probe generation path; task scheduling itself is _try_recovery at 221-241 but the probe logic cited lives at 154-199
line 32: 'Still ~5% of turns hung up to 5000ms ("death spiral")'

claims.claim: sometimes a request to a provider just hangs - no error, no timeout, nothing
claims.found: line 32: 'sometimes a request to a provider just hangs - no error, no timeout, nothing.'

claims.claim: cancel it and immediately fire a new request to the second-fastest
claims.found: line 34: 'cancel it and immediately fire a new request to the second-fastest'

claims.claim: compute historical standard deviation per deployment
claims.found: line 34: 'Compute historical standard deviation per deployment'

claims.claim: dynamic threshold based on what constitutes abnormal latency for that specific deployment
claims.found: line 34: 'set a dynamic threshold based on what constitutes abnormal latency for that specific deployment'

claims.claim: This system alone shaved >1000ms off P95 latency
claims.found: line 38: 'This system alone shaved >1000ms off P95 latency.'

claims.claim: mean + k·σ is the note's interpretation, not a source quote
claims.found: note writes '(i.e. mean + k·σ)' as parenthetical gloss; source says 'historical standard deviation' + 'dynamic threshold'; source takeaway line 43 also frames it '(mean+k·σ)'. Not falsely attributed.

claims.claim: LiveKit FallbackAdapter is failover-on-error not latency hedging on per-endpoint sigma; no clone implements mean+k·σ
claims.found: code only uses fixed attempt_timeout=5.0 and TimeoutError/APIError -> next LLM; no per-endpoint stddev or dynamic threshold anywhere in the file
